summary: Possible typosquat of 'keccak256' with author impersonation
details: Package name 'kecak256' is a one-character-deletion variant of the widely-used 'keccak256' package, and package.json copies the legitimate package's author field ('Miguel Mota'). The shipped code (kecak256.js) is a functionally correct keccak256 implementation that only requires bn.js, buffer, and keccak, and exposes a keccak256() function. There is no network I/O, no filesystem access, no child_process, no eval/Function, no obfuscation, and no lifecycle scripts. No installer-harm payload (exfiltration, dropper, silent-relay, credential distribution) is present. The combination of name-confusion and author impersonation creates real risk that developers install this package by mistake instead of the legitimate one, but absent a payload the intent is ambiguous and warrants human review rather than an automatic block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
